concerns.text: Task T006 (checksum verification) is marked as 'Depends on T005 completion', but T005 is also marked [P] (parallel-safe). T005 downloads data; T006 verifies it. These cannot run in parallel as T006 requires the artifact produced by T005. The [P] tag on T005 is misleading if it implies T006 can start simultaneously, or T006 should not be in the same parallel block. More critically, T006 depends on T005, so they are not parallel-safe relative to each other. The phase description says 'All Foundational tasks marked [P] can run in parallel', which is false for T005/T006.

Task T012a (Apply bandpass filter) is marked as 'Depends on T004, T005'. T005 downloads data, but T004 is just directory setup. The actual data loading logic is in T005. However, T013a (ICA) depends on T012a, T014a (Epoching) on T013a, etc. This chain is correct. BUT, T017a (Save outputs) depends on T016a (Exclusion logic). The exclusion logic (T016a) requires the behavioral metrics (T015a) which require epochs (T014a). This is a valid chain. However, the 'Independent Test' for US1 states the system can be tested by running the script on a single subject. The tasks T012a-T017a are all in one file `code/preprocess_eeg.py`. This is fine for a script, but the dependency chain is tight. No major ordering violation here, but the [P] tag on T010a/T011a (tests) is correct as they are separate files.

Task T021a (Load preprocessed epochs) depends on T017a (US1 output). This is correct. T022a (Define pairs) depends on T021a. T023a (Wavelets) depends on T022a. T024a (IPD) depends on T023a. T025a (Transforms) depends on T024a. T026a (wPLI) depends on T023a. T027a (Save) depends on T025a and T026a. T028a (Verify) depends on T027a. This chain is logically sound. However, T026a (wPLI) depends on T023a (Wavelets), which is correct. But T025a (Transforms) depends on T024a (IPD). The dependency graph is a DAG. No violation here.

Task T031a (Merge data) depends on T017a (US1) and T027a (US2). This is correct. T032a (Log-transform) depends on T031a. T033a (Fit LMM) depends on T032a. T034a (LRT) depends on T033a. T035a (Correction) depends on T034a. T036a (Permutation) depends on T035a. T037a (Directionality) depends on T036a. T038a (Save) depends on T037a. T039a (Report) depends on T038a. This chain is correct. However, the 'Independent Test' for US3 requires the LMM output. The tasks are sequential. No violation.

The comment 'Depends on T005 completion' on T006 is redundant if T006 is listed after T005 in a sequential phase, but it clarifies the dependency. However, the [P] tag on T005 is problematic if it implies T006 can run in parallel. The text 'All Foundational tasks marked [P] can run in parallel' is incorrect because T006 depends on T005. This is a documentation inconsistency that could lead to execution errors.
